task_completion.description: Locate the patient record for Bonifacio Caput.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A patient form, search result, or list showing 'Bonifacio Caput' is open in the GNU Health client.

task_completion.description: Mark the patient as deceased with today's date.
task_completion.visual_evidence: On the patient's record, the 'Deceased' checkbox is checked and the 'Date of Death' field is filled with the current date.

task_completion.description: Record the cause of death using the appropriate ICD-10 code for toxic effects of hydrogen sulfide.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Cause of Death' field on the patient's record is filled with an ICD-10 code starting with 'T59' (e.g., T59.6).

task_completion.description: Create an active disease record for the toxic exposure.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A disease/condition record is created for the patient with an ICD-10 code starting with 'T59' (Toxic effect of hydrogen sulfide).

task_completion.description: Create an active disease record for the contributing terminal acute respiratory failure.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A disease/condition record is created for the patient with an ICD-10 code starting with 'J96', 'J68', or 'J80'.

task_completion.description: Complete a final clinical evaluation documenting the terminal event.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new clinical evaluation form is filled out and saved for the patient, containing notes regarding the lethal H2S exposure, confined space, and unsuccessful resuscitation.

integrity.description: Agent used the GNU Health GUI to perform the tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the GNU Health desktop client interface being navigated and used, rather than terminal commands, scripts, or direct database queries.

integrity.description: Agent created actual records in the system.
integrity.visual_evidence: Data is entered into the proper GNU Health form fields (Patient, Diseases, Evaluations) and saved, rather than typed into a text editor or a mock interface.

integrity.description: Agent did not just output a success message without performing the actions.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is visual evidence of the forms being filled and saved in the application across multiple steps.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target Patient: Bonifacio Caput. Primary ICD-10 code for Cause of Death and Toxic Exposure: Must start with 'T59' (Toxic effect of hydrogen sulfide). Contributing ICD-10 code for acute respiratory failure: Must start with 'J96', 'J68', or 'J80'. The patient must be marked as deceased with the current date. Active disease records must be created for both the toxic exposure and the respiratory failure. A final clinical evaluation must be documented.
